title: Max Tegmark — Consciousness as a State of Matter (Collected)
subtitle: 4 papers on consciousness as a state of matter, the mathematical universe hypothesis, and the physics of information

summary.one_line: Tegmark's 'perceptronium' hypothesis — consciousness as a state of matter characterized by integrated information and self-awareness — provides a physical framework for understanding the upāya syst...
summary.scope: Covers perceptronium, the mathematical universe hypothesis, quantum decoherence in the brain.

sources.label: Max Tegmark — Collected Papers. 4 PDFs in /root/projects/blog/library/science/authors/tegmark/.

body.text: Tegmark defines perceptronium as 'the most general substance that feels self-aware' — a state of matter characterized by high integrated information and the capacity for self-modeling. This IS Śiva tattva (1), the condition of self-aware consciousness. The 'phase transitions' between forms of perceptronium IS the upāya system: a shift from low-integration (Āṇavopāya) to high-integration (Śāmbhavopāya) consciousness.
